features.name: Anonymous Book Search
features.description: Users can search the library catalog by title, author, ISBN, or genre without requiring authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/books/search returns paginated list of books
Searching by keyword (q param) matches title, author, or ISBN (case-insensitive)
Filtering by genre returns only books of that genre
Empty search returns all books sorted by title
Response is wrapped in standard ApiResponse envelope
Pagination works correctly (page, size, totalPages, totalElements)
Streamlit UI shows search bar + genre dropdown + paginated results grid
Each book card shows title, author, genre, year, description preview

features.name: Book Detail View
features.description: Users can view full details of a specific book including description, ISBN, and metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/books/{id} returns full book details
Returns 404 with error envelope if book not found
Streamlit UI shows book detail panel when a book card is clicked

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Users can create accounts and log in with username and password. Passwords are hashed (BCrypt) and stored in the local database.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/auth/register accepts username, password, confirmPassword
Registration validates username (3–50 chars, alphanumeric + underscore)
Registration validates password (min 8 chars) and confirmPassword match
Passwords are stored as BCrypt hashes (never plain text) in users table
Registration returns 201 with user id and username (no password in response)
Registration returns 409 if username already exists
POST /api/v1/auth/login accepts username and password
Login returns 200 with session token and user info on valid credentials
Login returns 401 on invalid username or password
Users table has id (UUID), username (unique), password_hash, created_at, updated_at
All responses use ApiResponse envelope
Streamlit UI shows Create Account and Login flows; displays welcome + Logout when logged in

features.name: Auth-Gated Landing Page
features.description: Redesign the logged-out experience: show a hero landing page with prominent Login/Create Account tabs and hide the book catalog from unauthenticated users.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users see ONLY the landing page — no search form, no book results
Landing page displays a centered hero title and welcoming subtitle
Landing page renders two tabs ('Log In' and 'Create Account') centered via a [1, 2, 1] column layout
Log In tab shows: 'Welcome back' heading, Username field, Password field, full-width Log In button
Create Account tab shows: 'Create your account' heading, Username field (max 50 chars), Password field, Confirm Password field, full-width Create Account button
Footer text 'Free to use. No credit card required.' is shown below the auth forms
Successful login sets session state and reruns into the full catalog experience
Successful registration shows a success message directing user to the Log In tab (no automatic login)
After logout, user returns to the landing page
Logged-in state is unchanged: header with welcome + logout, search form, paginated book grid
All existing API helper functions (search_books, register_user, login_user) are functionally unchanged
All session state keys retain the st_ prefix convention
All existing backend unit and integration tests continue to pass
Frontend tests updated to cover the new landing page layout

features.name: Prometheus & Grafana Observability
features.description: Local Prometheus and Grafana stack for metrics (PromQL) and dashboards, enabling agents and developers to observe app behavior and validate SLOs.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes GET /actuator/prometheus in Prometheus text format
Prometheus scrapes backend every 15s and shows target UP
Grafana runs on port 3000 with Prometheus as default data source
Docker Compose includes prometheus and grafana services on library-network
start.sh prints Grafana and Prometheus URLs in success banner
docs/RELIABILITY.md documents PromQL examples and agent prompts
AGENTS.md includes Prometheus/Grafana in component table
testSteps: Manual: curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/prometheus returns Prometheus format
Manual: Prometheus Targets show library-backend UP
Manual: Grafana Explore can run PromQL e.g. jvm_memory_used_bytes

features.name: Create Account Success UX
features.description: Replace the Create Account form with a success confirmation view after successful registration, with a 'Go to Log In' button that navigates the user to the Log In tab.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful account creation, the registration form is replaced by a success confirmation view
Success view displays 'Account Created!' heading and 'Your account has been created successfully.' subtitle
Success view displays a full-width 'Go to Log In' button
Clicking 'Go to Log In' clears the success state and switches to the Log In tab
On failed account creation, the form remains visible with st.error() messages
Session state key st_registration_success (bool) controls rendering
No form fields are visible after successful registration
All existing backend and frontend tests continue to pass

features.name: Email-Based Authentication with Email Verification
features.description: Replace username-based auth with email-based auth. Users must verify their email via a time-limited link before they can log in. Local SMTP via Mailpit; production-ready via environment variables. Database is stateless between container restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/auth/register accepts email, password, confirmPassword (not username)
Registration validates email format (@Email) and password (min 8 chars, confirm match)
Registration sends a verification email via SMTP with a time-limited link (default 15 min)
Registration returns 201 with user id, email, emailVerified=false
Registration returns 409 if email already registered
POST /api/v1/auth/login accepts email and password (not username)
Login returns 200 with JWT token and user info for verified users only
Login returns 403 with clear message if email is not verified
Login returns 401 on invalid email or password
GET /api/v1/auth/verify?token={token} activates the account and returns HTML success page
Expired or invalid verification tokens return an HTML error page
POST /api/v1/auth/resend-verification sends a new verification email (rate limited: 1 per 60s)
Resend endpoint always returns 200 regardless of email existence (prevents enumeration)
Verification emails are viewable in Mailpit web UI at http://localhost:8025
Database does NOT persist data across docker compose down/up (stateless via tmpfs)
All SMTP settings are environment-variable driven (MAIL_HOST, MAIL_PORT, MAIL_USERNAME, MAIL_PASSWORD, MAIL_SMTP_AUTH, MAIL_STARTTLS_ENABLED, MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS)
All responses use ApiResponse envelope (except GET /verify which returns HTML)
Streamlit UI shows Email fields (not Username) for registration and login
Post-registration shows 'Check your email' view with resend button
Login 403 error shows 'Email not verified' message with resend button
All auth-related actions are logged at appropriate levels (INFO for successful operations, WARN for verification failures, ERROR for email send failures)
All existing architecture tests continue to pass
start.sh prints Mailpit URL in success banner
